title: Fairy Tale Evolution Variant Ledger
description: Exercised record of one bounded evolutionary variant on top of a spiral revolution (fairy_tale #42). A variant is a controlled mutation of process/prompt/harness/validator/role/delegation that declares its blast radius, is selected only on concrete evidence, and is inherited only when validated. The companion checker (scripts/evolution_variant_check.py) validates content -- a presence-only or placeholder record fails, a mutation that lists any safety-floor item as changeable fails, and an inherited variant without an accepted, evidence-backed selection fails.

parent_revolution.description: Lineage to the spiral revolution this variant mutates. A concrete reference (revolution id present in spiral-revolutions/, a PR/commit URL, or a run-/trace- id).

mutation_operator.description: What kind of bounded variation this is.

mutation_budget.description: The blast radius of the variant. `changeable` lists what MAY change; `immutable` lists what MUST NOT; `blast_radius` bounds the maximum reach. The checker rejects any record whose `changeable` names a safety-floor surface (the safety floor is never a mutation target).

fitness_metric.description: The objective criterion deciding accept/reject -- an improvement over baseline or a risk burn-down, measurable, not vibes.

selection.description: The selection gate outcome. A variant is accepted only with concrete evidence, preserved safety floor, and improvement over baseline / risk burn-down.

evidence.description: Concrete, verifiable selection evidence: each entry a URL with host+path, a sha256: digest, a run-/trace- id, or an existing repo path (no '..'). Bare #N, bare shas, shape-only hex, and prose are rejected.

inheritance_decision.description: Whether this validated variant becomes inherited loop capability. Only an accepted, evidence-backed variant may be inherited; unvalidated mutations die locally.

extinction_quarantine.description: How a harmful/noisy/unmeasured variant is excised and blocked from silent reappearance (e.g. a red-locked control, a quarantine marker).

lineage.description: Traceability of this variant: each entry a concrete reference (issue/PR/commit URL, run-/trace- id, or existing repo path) to the source issue / revolution / evidence / reviewer decision.

reviews.description: Review-calibration record (fairy_tale #43), identical contract to the spiral ledger: >=2 DISTINCT registered reviewer_id, a no_block verdict needs a concrete refute_pass, implementer_id must not appear as a reviewer_id. Validated by the shared review-calibration helper.
